title: The view from my window
summary: Describe what you can see from your window.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about what you see from your window. Describe the buildings, sky, street, or nature outside. Use present simple and there is / there are.
hintStarters: From my window, I can see…
There is a … in front of the building.
In the morning, the view looks…
sampleText: From my window in my flat in Edinburgh, I can see a narrow cobblestone street. Opposite my building, there is a small bakery. In the morning, a warm light comes from the bakery window and people go in and out with bread. There are two tall trees on the pavement. In autumn, their leaves are orange and red. A few pigeons always sit on the rooftop across the street. They walk around and look for food. The street is quiet in the early morning, but it gets busy after eight o'clock. I can sometimes see children in school uniforms walking past. The sky above the street is often grey because Edinburgh is a cloudy city. But when the sun comes out, the old stone buildings look very beautiful. I like this view because it feels alive. There is always something to look at. I sit at the window with my tea and I watch the street for a few minutes every morning.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: con đường hẹp lát đá cuội
Đối diện tòa nhà của tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: warm light comes from the bakery window
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ánh sáng ấm áp chiếu từ cửa sổ tiệm bánh mì
yên tĩnh vào buổi sáng sớm
trở nên bận rộn sau tám giờ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: There is / There are for describing a scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'there is' + danh từ số ít, 'there are' + danh từ số nhiều: 'there is a small bakery', 'there are two tall trees'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' for singular nouns and 'there are' for plural: 'there is a small bakery', 'there are two tall trees on the pavement'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Get + adjective for change
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'get + adjective' diễn tả sự thay đổi trạng thái: 'it gets busy after eight o'clock' = trở nên bận rộn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'get + adjective' shows a change of state: 'it gets busy after eight o'clock'.

sampleTranslationVi: Từ cửa sổ căn hộ ở Edinburgh, tôi có thể nhìn thấy một con đường hẹp lát đá cuội. Đối diện tòa nhà của tôi là một tiệm bánh mì nhỏ. Vào buổi sáng, ánh sáng ấm áp chiếu từ cửa sổ tiệm bánh và người ta đi ra đi vào với bánh mì trên tay. Có hai cây cao trên vỉa hè. Vào mùa thu, lá cây có màu cam và đỏ. Vài con chim bồ câu luôn ngồi trên mái nhà bên kia đường. Chúng đi lại và tìm kiếm thức ăn. Con đường yên tĩnh vào buổi sáng sớm, nhưng trở nên bận rộn sau tám giờ. Đôi khi tôi thấy những đứa trẻ mặc đồng phục học sinh đi ngang qua. Bầu trời phía trên con đường thường xám xịt vì Edinburgh là thành phố nhiều mây. Nhưng khi mặt trời ló dạng, những tòa nhà đá cũ trông rất đẹp. Tôi thích cảnh nhìn này vì nó cảm thấy sống động. Lúc nào cũng có điều gì đó để nhìn. Tôi ngồi bên cửa sổ với tách trà và ngắm phố vài phút mỗi buổi sáng.

mcQuestions.prompt: What city does the writer live in?
What is opposite the writer's building?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Opposite my building, there is a small bakery'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What colour are the leaves on the trees in autumn?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'their leaves are orange and red' in autumn.

mcQuestions.prompt: When does the street get busy?
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the time the quiet ends.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why is the sky often grey?
options: Because there is a factory nearby.
Because Edinburgh is a cloudy city.
Because it always rains in the morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'the sky … is often grey because Edinburgh is a cloudy city'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which uses 'there is / there are' correctly?
options: There are a small bakery opposite.
There is a small bakery opposite.
There is two tall trees on the pavement.
There are a tree on the pavement.

mcQuestions.prompt: What do the pigeons do on the rooftop?
options: They walk around and look for food.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'They walk around and look for food'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer do at the window each morning?
options: They paint a picture of the street.
They open it and shout at people.
They sit with their tea and watch the street.
They call a friend on the phone.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I sit at the window with my tea and I watch the street'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means the street is not wide?

options: The street gets busy after eight.
The street is getting busy after eight.
The street get busy after eight.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple: 'it gets busy' for a regular daily change.
mcQuestions.hint: This describes what happens every day.

mcQuestions.prompt: What makes the old stone buildings look beautiful?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'when the sun comes out, the old stone buildings look very beautiful'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why might people enjoy watching a busy street from their window, even without doing anything special?
